name: Inositol Hexanicotinate
summary: Inositol hexanicotinate is a flush-free niacin form that is better tolerated but does not reliably reproduce nicotinic acid's lipid effects. Evidence supports caution against treating it as an equivalent niacin substitute.
description: Ester of inositol and nicotinic acid marketed as flush-free niacin; lipid effects appear weak or ineffective compared with nicotinic acid.
safetyNotes: In a six-week trial, 1,500 mg/day inositol hexanicotinate was well tolerated but showed no lipid benefit over placebo and no measurable bioavailability in a small pharmacokinetic subset. Long-term, pregnancy, breastfeeding, liver-disease, kidney-disease, and medication-interaction safety remain insufficient; safety claims for other niacin forms should not be transferred to this form.
